channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Alabama carries out nation’s third nitrogen gas execution on a man for a hitchhiker's killing
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An Alabama man convicted in the 1994 killing of a hitchhiker cursed at the prison warden and made obscene gestures with his hands shortly before he was put to death Thursday evening in the nation’s third execution using nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Passengers duct tape unruly man who tried to open door on American Airlines plane
item.description: A group of passengers aboard a Dallas-bound flight restrained a man with duct tape after he claimed to be the "captain" and attempted to open the cabin door of an American Airlines plane midair, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know about a storm bringing high winds, heavy rain, snow to California and Pacific Northwest
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — One of the strongest storms on the West Coast in decades knocked out power for thousands of people, unleashed strong winds that toppled trees and left two dead in Washington before making its way through Oregon to Northern California where on Thursday it dropped heavy snow and r
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Historically bad year for dolphin strandings on Cape Cod has scientists looking for answers
item.description: Scientists say an unprecedentedly bad year for beached dolphins on Cape Cod might have to do with warming waters changing the availability of the animals’ food.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Attorneys want the US Supreme Court to say Mississippi's felony voting ban is cruel and unusual
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — The U.S. Supreme Court should overturn Mississippi's Jim Crow-era practice of removing voting rights from people convicted of certain felonies, including nonviolent crimes such as forgery and timber theft, attorneys say in new court papers.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kit Kats to come in new shape for the first time ever in America
item.description: The Hershey Company has debuted Kit Kats in the shape of something other than a traditional Kit Kat "finger.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenTable names 2024's 'Top 100 Restaurants' in America
item.description: OpenTable's analysts based their findings on more than 14 million diner reviews, along with diner ratings, reservation demand, and percentage of five-star reviews, among other factors.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pathologist disputes finding that Marine veteran's chokehold caused subway rider's death
item.description: A forensic pathologist disputes a New York City medical examiner's determination that former Marine Daniel Penny killed another man by placing him in a chokehold on a subway train last year.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gaetz withdraws from attorney general consideration
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) has withdrawn his nomination to be Attorney General for President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KTLA 5

item.title: New study shows voting for Native Americans is harder than ever
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY, Okla. (AP) — A new study has found that systemic barriers to voting on tribal lands contribute to substantial disparities in Native American turnout, particularly for presidential elections.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOJ asks judge to order Google to sell Chrome
item.description: The DOJ’s proposal would require Google to divest from Chrome and prohibit the search giant from owning another browser for five years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: St. Louis was once known as Mound City for its many Native American mounds. Just one remains
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — What is now St. Louis was once home to more than 100 mounds constructed by Native Americans — so many that St. Louis was once known as “Mound City.” Settlers tore most of them down, and just one remains.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, Fox 5

item.title: No charges filed against man arrested after teens set up sting
item.description: A 46-year-old California man ambushed by vigilante teens after he allegedly provided a girl with nude photos of himself and tried to arrange a sexual encounter with her is, so far, not facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: US charges one of the world's richest people with fraud
item.description: Indian businessman Gautam Adani, one of the world's richest people, was charged by the U.S. on allegations of fraud.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal Reserve's likely slowdown in rate cuts could disappoint borrowers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Just a few weeks ago, the path ahead for the Federal Reserve looked straightforward: With inflation cooling and the job market slowing, the Fed appeared on track to steadily cut interest rates.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man convicted of killing Laken Riley sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — The Venezuelan man convicted of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole in a case that became a flashpoint in the national debate over immigration.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Maryland man allegedly shot, killed teenage stepson for not completing chores: police
item.description: A Maryland stepdad allegedly shot and killed his 15-year-old stepson on Nov. 20, 2024, after a dispute for not completing his chores, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Human smuggling trial witness says he was separated from a family hours before they froze to death
item.description: FERGUS FALLS, Minn. (AP) — An Indian national who survived a treacherous trek across the U.S.-Canada border in blizzard conditions testified Wednesday that he got separated from a family of four shortly before they froze to death.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Unhappy with the 2024 election results? This Italian village is offering $1 homes to Americans
item.description: Americans who are unhappy with the 2024 election results and have fantasized about leaving the country could turn that dream into a reality thanks to a recent offer from an Italian village.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump names ambassador to NATO
item.description: President-elect Trump has named his former acting attorney general, Matthew Whitaker, as the next ambassador to NATO, selecting him to fill the role at a crucial time amid Russia's war in Ukraine and tensions over defense spending across the Western security alliance.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, ABC 4

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Most say they probably won't get updated COVID vaccine: Survey
item.description: It has been over four years since the U.S. began dealing with COVID-19, with the spread of the virus causing mass shutdowns within multiple industries and people around the world being forced to stay in their homes.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Susan Smith denied parole 30 years after drowning sons by rolling car into South Carolina lake
item.description: Susan Smith made national headlines in 1994 after she rolled her car into a lake with both of her children strapped in their seats.
item.dc:creator: Dustin George, Fox 5

item.title: Amid X exodus, Bluesky CEO says 1M people per day joined in past week
item.description: The social media platform Bluesky has emerged as a popular alternative for users departing Elon Musk's X and other platforms, with nearly 1 million people joining each day over the past week, Bluesky CEO Jay Graber said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Man convicted of killing nursing student Laken Riley sentenced to life in prison without parole
item.description: The Venezuelan man convicted of killing Georgia nursing student Laken Riley has been sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Delta to begin serving Shake Shack on select flights
item.description: Travelers soon will be able to enjoy Shake Shack's famous burgers on select Delta flights beginning next month, with plans to expand throughout the U.S. in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Jay Leno falls 60 feet down hill, hits face on rock
item.description: Wearing an eye patch, Jay Leno was bruised but in good spirits when he arrived at a comedy show in Beverly Hills on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KRON4

item.title: Italian village offering $1 homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: CMA Awards 2024: Everything to know about Country Music's Biggest Night
item.description: Get ready for the "biggest night" in country music with everything you need to know for the 58th Annual CMA Awards.
item.dc:creator: Sebastian Posey, KRON4

item.title: Country Music Association Awards: who to watch
item.description: Post Malone and Morgan Wallen’s collaboration “I Had Some Help” could make both men big winners at Wednesday night's Country Music Association Awards.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON AP Entertainment Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has called for dismantling the Education Department. Here's what that would mean
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Throughout his campaign, President-elect Donald Trump heaped scorn on the federal Department of Education, describing it as being infiltrated by “ radicals, zealots and Marxists.” He has picked Linda McMahon, a former wrestling executive, to lead the department.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Barbie released in honor of EGOT winner Rita Moreno
item.description: Toy maker Mattel is releasing a doll honoring Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony (EGOT) winner Rita Moreno, a prominent Latina actor.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Mattel releases doll honoring EGOT winner Rita Moreno
item.description: Toy maker Mattel is releasing a doll honoring Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony (EGOT) winner Rita Moreno, a prominent Latina actor.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: 2 hospitalized after California theme park riders stranded mid-air
item.description: Two thrill-seekers were hospitalized Monday after they became stranded for more than two hours on a malfunctioning ride at Knott’s Berry Farm in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: National Geographic names 2024 Pictures of the Year
item.description: Emperor penguins taking the plunge, an Arkansas family experiencing a total solar eclipse, and a scientist holding a rare white rhino fetus are among the most striking and important images captured this year, according to National Geographic.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: 2 are killed and 1 is critically wounded in New York City knife rampage. A suspect is in custody
item.description: Officials say a man has carried out a string of stabbings across a swath of Manhattan that killed two people and critically wounded a third. The 51-year-old suspect is in police custody after Monday morning's attacks.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, WFLA, NBC
